---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 12:40 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Litware Inc

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 13:03 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into consumption trend.

[00:07:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:09:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:12:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:19:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:23:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:30:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:35:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.
